name: PostHog
tagline: Product analytics an agent can query in natural language.
description: PostHog's MCP server exposes 50+ tools: run HogQL, build insights and dashboards, manage feature flags. Agents can answer 'what changed in signups last week?' against real product data.

methods.label: Personal API key
methods.note: Scoped keys created per-use; used by both the MCP server and REST API.

auth.guide:
Create a personal API key under [Settings → Personal API keys](https://app.posthog.com/settings/user-api-keys) (`phx_…`). Choose the **MCP Server** preset when creating it — that grants exactly the scopes the MCP tools need.

**MCP (hosted):**

```bash
claude mcp add --transport http posthog https://mcp.posthog.com/mcp \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer phx_your_key"
```

**REST API:** the same key works against the private API:

```bash
curl https://us.posthog.com/api/projects/ \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $POSTHOG_API_KEY"
```

Note the **region split**: US Cloud uses `us.posthog.com`, EU Cloud uses `eu.posthog.com` — match your instance or you'll get auth errors with a valid key. Project API keys (`phc_…`) are for event *capture* only and won't work here.
